story.title: COVID-19 Oral History with Judy Taylor
story.description: The interview primarily discusses the role and work of Judy Taylor, Director of the Idaho Commission on Aging, during the COVID-19 pandemic. The conversation explores her career trajectory, from starting as a nurse to her current position, focusing on the challenges faced and strategies implemented in response to the pandemic. Key topics include the Commission's programs like Adult Protective Services and the Ombudsman Program, adaptations made using federal funding, communication challenges, and initiatives to combat social isolation among the elderly. Additionally, Taylor reflects on her participation in the Vaccine Advisory Committee, emphasizing ethical considerations in vaccine distribution. The discussion also highlights the pandemic's personal impact and broader reflections on state leadership and the need for preparedness in health crises, emphasizing Idaho's commitment to serving its elderly population effectively.
